records.ety: [F., fr. L. prehensio, prensio, a seizing, arresting, fr. prehendre, prendere, to lay hold of, to seize. See Prehensile, and cf. Prize, n., Misprision.]

defs: A place where persons are confined, or restrained of personal liberty; hence, a place or state o<?/ confinement, restraint, or safe custody.
quotes: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may praise thy name.
The tyrant Aeolus, . . . With power imperial, curbs the struggling winds, And sounding tempests in dark prisons binds.

defs: Specifically, a building for the safe custody or confinement of criminals and others committed by lawful authority.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Prisoned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Prisoning.]

defs: To imprison; to shut up in, or as in, a prison; to confine; to restrain from liberty.
quotes: The prisoned eagle dies for rage.
His true respect will prison false desire.
Sir William Crispyn with the duke was led Together prisoned.
